content.question: Does the regional variation in SNI prevalence (5.4% to 14% across counties) reflect genuine epidemiological differences, or local differences in diagnostic/administrative practice, expert committee decision-making, or specialist service availability?
content.reduction_path: Audit diagnostic protocols and expert committee decision records across high- and low-prevalence counties, standardize assessment instruments, and compare classifications of the same cases across committees to isolate practice variance from true prevalence differences.
